name: Modify Alert
summary: Modifies an existing pending price alert. Send a POST request with a jData JSON body (the original alert parameters with the changed values) and the session jKey; returns a success/failure status with the alert id.

request_params.name: jData
request_params.description: Should send json object with fields in below list.

json_fields.name: uid
json_fields.description: User id of the logged in user.

json_fields.name: ai_t
json_fields.description: Alert Type, should be original alert type, can't be modified.

json_fields.name: d
json_fields.description: Data to be compared with LTP.

json_fields.name: remarks
json_fields.description: Any message Entered during order entry.

response_fields.name: request_time
response_fields.description: This will be present only in a successful response.

response_fields.name: emsg
response_fields.description: This will be present only in case of errors. That is : 1) Invalid Input 2) Session Expired

notes:
- The curl example uses 'https://BaseURL/ModifyAlert' as a placeholder; the real endpoint is https://piconnect.flattrade.in/PiConnectAPI/ModifyAlert.
- The jData payload is sent as a form-encoded body in the form jData={...}&jKey=... (not a raw JSON body), even though Content-Type is application/json in the doc's example.
- ai_t (Alert Type) must be the ORIGINAL alert type — it cannot be changed by a modify call.
- The jData field table spans pages 61-62: uid*/tsym*/exch*/ai_t*/al_id on page 61, then validity*/d/remarks* on page 62. validity Possible value = "DAY or GTT".
- Required fields (trailing '*' in the table): uid, tsym, exch, ai_t, validity, remarks. NOT required (no '*'): al_id, d.
- The sample curl shows an extra "actid" field not listed in the field table; the field table lists uid/tsym/exch/ai_t/al_id/validity/d/remarks.
- Success response is a JSON array; failure response is a JSON object. Success stat is "Oi Replaced".
- The cleaned-text JSON uses curly/smart quotes (e.g. “al_id”) in places — reproduced verbatim from the source.
issues:
- The cleaned text renders some JSON keys with smart/curly quotes (e.g. “al_id”) rather than straight quotes; kept verbatim as decoded.
- The request curl shows "actid" which is not documented in the jData field table (likely copy-paste from another endpoint); included verbatim in sample_request but not added as a documented field.
- ai_t vs al_id naming: the doc uses ai_t for Alert Type and al_id for Alert Id; both appear and are distinct fields.
